title: Ensure audit log files owner is configured

prose.en: Ensure audit log files owner is configured

description.en: Audit log files contain information about the system and system activity.

rationale.en: Access to audit records can reveal system and configuration data to attackers, potentially compromising its confidentiality.

audit.en:
Run the following command to configure the audit log files to be owned by the `root` user:

```
# [ -f /etc/audit/auditd.conf ] && find "$(dirname $(awk -F "=" '/^\s*log_file/ {print $2}' /etc/audit/auditd.conf | xargs))" -type f ! -user root -exec chown root {} +
```

remediation.en:
Run the following command to configure the audit log files to be owned by the `root` user:

```
# [ -f /etc/audit/auditd.conf ] && find "$(dirname $(awk -F "=" '/^\s*log_file/ {print $2}' /etc/audit/auditd.conf | xargs))" -type f ! -user root -exec chown root {} +
```
